title: Oil Change
steps: Warm engine for 5 minutes, then shut off
Place drain pan under oil pan and remove drain plug
Allow oil to drain completely (5-10 minutes)
Replace drain plug gasket with new one
Tighten drain plug to 44 Nm (33 ft-lb)
Remove old oil filter using ST 498547000 or filter wrench
Apply thin coat of new oil to new filter gasket
Install new filter hand-tight, then turn 3/4 additional turn
Fill engine oil through filler cap to upper level mark (4.0 L)
Start engine, run for 1 minute, shut off and recheck level
Top up if necessary between L and F marks on dipstick

title: Timing Belt Replacement
steps: Disconnect battery ground cable
Remove V-belts (alternator, A/C, power steering)
Remove crankshaft pulley using ST 499977300
Remove belt covers (RH and LH)
Remove timing belt guide (MT vehicles only)
Mark timing belt rotation direction if reusing
Align crank sprocket mark (a) with cylinder block notch (b)
Verify cam sprocket alignment marks match cylinder head surfaces
Remove belt idler No. 2, then belt idler No. 1
Remove timing belt
Remove automatic belt tension adjuster assembly
Press adjuster rod down with 294 N pressure, insert 2mm stopper pin
Install new tensioner adjuster assembly - torque to 25 Nm
Install belt idler No. 1 - torque to 39 Nm
Align camshaft sprockets using ST1 and ST2
Install new timing belt matching alignment marks, ensure correct rotation
Install belt idler No. 2 - torque to 39 Nm
Remove stopper pin from tensioner adjuster
Install timing belt guide (MT only), check 1.0mm clearance
Install belt covers, crankshaft pulley, and V-belts
Rotate engine 2 full turns and verify all alignment marks

title: Coolant Flush
steps: Allow engine to cool completely - radiator is pressurized when hot
Lift vehicle, remove under cover
Place drain pan under radiator, remove drain cock
Remove radiator cap to speed draining
Drain coolant completely from radiator and engine block
Close drain cock, fill radiator with clean water
Run engine at 2,000-3,000 rpm for 5 minutes to flush
Drain flush water and repeat if still discolored
Close drain cock, fill with Subaru Genuine Coolant mix to filler neck
Fill reservoir tank to FULL level
Warm engine at 2,000-3,000 rpm for 5+ minutes
Check radiator level as thermostat opens, top up as needed
Check reservoir level, add coolant to upper level mark
Install radiator cap and reservoir cap securely

title: Brake Pads (Front & Rear)
steps: Loosen wheel lug nuts, raise vehicle and support on jack stands
Remove wheel
Inspect pad thickness through caliper inspection hole
Remove caliper guide pin bolts (lower bolt first)
Swing caliper up and support with wire - do not hang by brake hose
Remove old pads, outer shim, inner shim, and pad clips
Clean caliper bracket/support with brake cleaner
Inspect disc rotor thickness and runout against service limits
Push caliper piston back using C-clamp (check fluid reservoir level first)
Install new pad clips on support bracket
Install new inner shim, pad (inside), pad (outside), and outer shim
Lower caliper over new pads and install guide pin bolts
Torque guide pin bolts to 37 Nm (front) or per spec (rear)
Reinstall wheel, torque lug nuts
Pump brake pedal several times before driving to seat pads
Check and top up brake fluid reservoir

title: Spark Plugs
steps: Disconnect battery ground cable
RH side: Remove air intake duct and resonator chamber
LH side: Disconnect washer motor, remove washer tank bolts
Remove spark plug cords by pulling on the boot, not the cord
Remove spark plugs with spark plug socket (16mm deep)
Inspect old plugs for fouling, damage, and wear
Check new plug gap with feeler gauge, adjust if needed
Install new spark plugs hand-tight first to prevent cross-threading
Torque spark plugs to 21 Nm (15 ft-lb) - dry threads only
If threads are oiled, reduce torque by approximately 1/3
Reconnect spark plug cords to correct positions (firing order 1-3-2-4)
Reinstall resonator chamber (torque to 32 Nm), air intake duct
Reinstall washer tank (LH side), reconnect battery

title: Air Filter Replacement
interval: Every 20,000 km or 12 months (inspect every 10,000 km)
steps: Open hood and locate air cleaner case on top of engine
Release clips (B) above the air cleaner case
Remove bolts (A) securing air cleaner case to stays
Separate upper air cleaner case (case B) from lower (case A)
Remove air cleaner element from case
Inspect element - replace if excessively damaged or dirty
Clean inside of both air cleaner case halves
Install new air cleaner element into lower case
Reassemble upper case, fasten with clips after inserting lower tab
Reinstall securing bolts

title: Manual Transmission Fluid Change

specs.label: * Note
specs.value: Community-standard value; not explicitly found in manual

steps: Raise vehicle and support on jack stands, remove under cover
Place drain pan under transmission
Clean area around drain and fill plugs
Remove fill plug first (to ensure it can be removed before draining)
Remove drain plug and allow fluid to drain completely
Inspect drain plug magnet for metal particles
Install drain plug with new washer, torque to spec
Fill transmission through fill plug hole with GL-5 gear oil
Fill until fluid begins to seep from fill hole (3.5 L total)
Install fill plug with new washer, torque to spec
Lower vehicle and test-drive, checking all gears engage smoothly

title: Clutch Replacement

specs.label: Clutch Disc O.D. x I.D. x Thickness

steps: Disconnect battery ground cable
Remove air intake duct and air cleaner assembly
Disconnect clutch cable or hydraulic line
Support engine with lifting device and wire ropes
Raise vehicle, remove front wheels and under cover
Drain transmission fluid
Remove front exhaust pipe
Remove propeller shaft (AWD models)
Disconnect all electrical connectors from transmission
Support transmission with jack, remove mounting bolts
Carefully lower and remove transmission
Mark clutch cover position relative to flywheel
Gradually loosen cover bolts in star pattern, remove clutch cover and disc
Inspect flywheel surface for scoring, heat damage, and runout
Inspect release bearing for noise and smooth rotation, replace if worn
Install new clutch disc using alignment tool (spline side toward transmission)
Install new clutch cover, align marks, torque bolts gradually to 15.7 Nm
Apply small amount of grease to input shaft splines
Install release bearing on release lever
Reinstall transmission, torque mounting bolts to spec
Reconnect all components in reverse order of removal
Fill transmission with GL-5 fluid (3.5 L)
Adjust clutch pedal free play, bleed hydraulic system if equipped

title: Differential Fluid Change

specs.label: * Note
specs.value: Rear diff capacity and plug torque are community-standard values

steps: Raise vehicle and support securely on jack stands
Place drain pan under rear differential
Clean area around drain and fill plugs on rear diff housing
Remove fill plug first (17mm hex) to ensure it can be removed
Remove drain plug and drain old fluid completely
Inspect drain plug magnet for excessive metal particles
Clean and reinstall drain plug with new crush washer
Fill rear differential through fill hole with GL-5 75W-90 gear oil
Fill until fluid seeps from fill hole (approximately 0.8 L)
Install fill plug with new crush washer
Note: Front differential shares fluid with manual transmission
For front diff service, perform transmission fluid change procedure
Lower vehicle and test-drive, listening for whine or noise
